title: NOC 42201 – Social and Community Service Workers | GTR Immigration
description: Social and Community Service Workers under NOC 42201 play a critical role in supporting vulnerable individuals, families, and communities across Canada. Classified under TEER 2, this occupation involves delivering front-line support services in social assistance, mental health, youth services, rehabilitation, and community development. These professionals work closely with social workers, psychologists, healthcare professionals, and government agencies to improve client outcomes and strengthen communities.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER2 – Occupations usually require a college diploma or apprenticeship training of two or more yearsMajor Group42 – Front-line public protection services and paraprofessional occupationsSub-Major Group422 – Paraprofessional occupations in legal, social, community, and education servicesMinor Group4220 – Paraprofessional occupationsNOC Code42201

duties: Social and Community Service Workers may:
Review client background and prepare intake reports
Develop action plans and provide support
Refer clients to legal, medical, housing, employment, or financial services
Counsel clients in group homes and halfway houses
Supervise client activities and assist with reintegration planning
Implement life skills and substance abuse programs
Provide crisis intervention and emergency support
Monitor client progress and evaluate treatment effectiveness
Maintain contact with other social service and healthcare providers
employment_requirements: Completion of a college or university program in:Social workChild and youth carePsychologySocial sciencesHealth-related disciplines
Previous volunteer or support experience may substitute for formal education in some cases
Membership in a provincial regulatory body may be required in certain provinces
Practical experience in social service environments is highly valued.
salary_prospects: Social and Community Service Workers typically earn between:
$32,300 – $68,700 per year,depending on experience, province, and specialization.
Northern territories often offer higher compensation.
job_outlook: This is one of the most stable and socially essential occupations in Canada.
labour_market_demand: Social and Community Service Workers are employed in:
This represents strong and sustained demand nationwide.
Expansion of youth and family support programs
Opportunities in government and non-profit sectors
Career growth into supervisory or specialized roles
Strong job stability in social services sector
Social and Community Service Workers are particularly in demand in Ontario, Alberta, British Columbia, and Northern territories.
pathways: Social and Community Service Workers fall under TEER 2 skilled occupations, potentially qualifying for:
